refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is solution-neutral, specifying what must be achieved without prescribing how. Therefore, it is atomic and does not require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Use error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design communication system with redundant data paths to mitigate packet loss
Incorporate real-time network quality monitoring and feedback control to dynamically optimize teleoperation data flow
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network condition monitoring accuracy
Interaction between adaptive protocol and error correction mechanisms if combined
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain packet loss limits by adapting transmission parameters, which can be modularized independently from other system functions. This supports functional independence and reduces coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable network condition metrics to inform adaptation
Risk that adaptation latency may impact teleoperation responsiveness
Potential complexity in protocol design to meet strict packet loss targets under variable conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the network condition monitoring as a separate functional requirement or sub-FR to clarify boundaries and support modular DP design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network communication protocols and adaptive transmission control

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to send and receive data packets and monitor transmission quality

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network connection for adjusting transmission parameters

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol is core to network handling; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module is broader and less focused on communication protocols, leading to significant diffusion.
Teleoperation uses communication but does not primarily manage adaptive protocols, causing moderate diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not communication protocol management, resulting in poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive communication protocol DP, as it directly manages network communication and transmission parameters, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface management to avoid tight coupling.
If adaptive protocol logic overlaps with Teleoperation control logic, coordination is needed to prevent duplicated functionality.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by implementing an adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters, ensuring packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit, thus fulfilling the teleoperation communication quality requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface management to avoid tight coupling.
If adaptive protocol logic overlaps with Teleoperation control logic, coordination is needed to prevent duplicated functionality.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion for the adaptive communication protocol DP, with a containment score of 8 and diffusion score of 2, indicating appropriate architectural packaging and ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface under agreed network conditions, ensuring compliance with the ≤1% packet loss acceptance criterion.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, as this module is responsible for network communication protocols and adaptive transmission control, aligning well with the FR.

recommended_revisions: Establish clear interface contracts and protocols between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to prevent duplication of adaptive protocol logic.
Implement rigorous interface management for Telemetry and Network Link to minimize coupling and maintain modularity.
Consider documenting coordination mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation to handle overlapping responsibilities effectively.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and properly allocated to the implementation module with manageable diffusion risks. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and architectural packaging is appropriate. Given the high safety criticality, the recommended interface management and coordination measures will mitigate identified risks without necessitating further decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: To implement the recommended interface contracts and rigorous interface management between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to prevent duplication and minimize coupling.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol DP fully satisfies the teleoperation communication quality FR.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary for the DP.
Interface management and coordination mechanisms can effectively mitigate diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential tight coupling due to dependencies on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
Possible duplication of adaptive protocol logic overlapping with Teleoperation control logic.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss metrics under operational conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and coordination mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
